text.title: OpenAI Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `fine-tuning-openai-dpo` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: DPO MUST start from an SFT fine-tune base, never from the foundation model directly.

rationale.source: Rafailov et al. 2023 DPO paper; OpenAI DPO docs

rule.rationale: DPO without SFT is unstable and rarely converges; OpenAI requires an SFT base for the dpo job type.

rule.statement: Require ≥500 preference pairs (≥2000 for production-grade); reject jobs below floor.

rationale.source: Rafailov et al. 2023; HuggingFace TRL DPO guide

rule.rationale: Below 500 pairs DPO overfits to label noise.

rule.statement: Pair labelling must achieve ≥0.6 Cohen's kappa (or 80% agreement) on a 100-pair audit subset.
rule.rationale: Low-agreement pairs introduce noise indistinguishable from signal.

rule.statement: Start beta=0.1; raise to 0.3 if model drifts too far from SFT; lower to 0.05 if preferences barely shift.
rule.rationale: beta controls the KL penalty to SFT; default 0.1 is empirically robust across tasks.

rule.statement: Block deployment until preference_rate > 0.55 on held-out pairs (LLM-as-judge or human).
rule.rationale: Without preference eval the model could be worse than SFT on the metric DPO targeted.

rule.statement: Same labelling guideline + same labeller pool across all pairs; never mix sources without per-source flag.
rule.rationale: Mixed-source labelling produces non-stationary preferences; DPO drifts toward majority source.
